revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:福長神社]][[zh:福长神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Fukunaga Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Fukunaga-jinja (Kyoto) shaden.JPG |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}[[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[Kamigyō Ward]], Fukunaga-chō, 538
{{small|Near Muromachi-dōri and Mushanokōji-dōri}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.027925|N|135.758139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Fukui-no-Kami
Tsunagai-no-Kami
[[Inari-no-Kami]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Major Shrine, 2 enshrined deities) |established = Unknown |main_hall = Small shrine |other_names = Fukunaga Inari |location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|qid=Q1321824|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}} }} '''Fukunaga Shrine''' (福長神社, Fukunaga-jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Fukunaga-chō, Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja|京都市上京区福長町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukunaga-chō, Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with the Rank of a Major Shrine for two enshrined deities. The shrine is also known as "Fukunaga Inari" {{small|(福長稲荷)}}.Shrine Description Plate (Kyoto City). == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''Fukui-no-Kami''' (福井神) * '''Tsunagai-no-Kami''' (綱長井神) * '''[[Inari-no-Kami]]''' (稲荷神) – a later addition. The name "Fukunaga" derives from the names of Fukui-no-Kami and Tsunagai-no-Kami. These two deities are among the "Five Deities of Zama" (座摩巫deity五座) mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, which were originally enshrined in the western precincts of the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|qid=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} (Department of Divinities) within the Heian Palace. They are associated with wells, believed to have originally protected palace water sources. == History == == Overview == The shrine was listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} of 927 as part of the Five Deities of Zama, indicating its significance. The deities were originally enshrined in the Jingikan precincts and were classified as Major Shrines (大社), receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami Festival|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}.{{Cite web|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1102|title=Fukui-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Database|access-date=2025-01-12}} According to shrine tradition, the deities were moved to their current location during the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1592) under {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, possibly linked to the construction or dismantling of the {{ill|Jurakudai|en|Jurakudai|qid=Q910041|lt=Jurakudai|1=Jurakudai}}. However, maps such as the "Rakuchū Rakugai-zu Byōbu" (洛中洛外図屏風) suggest that Fukunaga Shrine already existed in this location by 1574. The shrine was damaged during the {{ill|Great Tenmei Fire|ja|天明の大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tenmei Fire|1=Tenmei Fire|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11442674}} of 1788 and has remained a small structure since then. === Historical Rank === * 859 CE (1st year of Jōgan): Fukui-no-Kami and Tsunagai-no-Kami were elevated from no Rank to Junior Fourth Rank, U11er Grade (従四位上), as noted in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}.{{Cite web|url=http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1103|title=Tsunagai-no-Kami – Kokugakuin University Database|access-date=2025-01-12}} == See Also == * {{ill|Zama Deities|ja|座摩神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|2=ja|3=座摩神|4=zh|5=坐摩巫祭神|1=Ikasuri no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikasuri no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1102 Fukui-no-Kami Database Entry – Kokugakuin University] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1103 Tsunagai-no-Kami Database Entry – Kokugakuin University] {{Shinto Footer}} [[File:Fukunaga-jinja (Kyoto) shaden.JPG|280px]] [[File:Fukunaga-jinja (Kyoto) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii Gate}}]] {{translated page|ja|福長神社|version=65461986|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:福長神社]] {{Commons category|Fukunaga-jinja}} [[zh:福长神社]] {{wikidata link|Q18339120}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha_in_the_Imperial_Palace]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]]
